title: Modbus Register Value Change Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: IEC 62443 FR 6.2 Continuous monitoring ‚Äî register-value change tracking is the application-layer evidence of continuous monitoring on Modbus-based automation systems.
NERC CIP-010-4 R1 Configuration change management ‚Äî Modbus register changes reflect control-system configuration adjustments that must be recorded under R1.
API RP 1164 3rd edition 6.2.1 Logging and monitoring ‚Äî Modbus register tracking is an explicit operational technology logging control for pipeline SCADA.

app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884)
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json" matched=true
| where func IN ("0x06","6","0x10","16")
| eval reg_key=destination_h."|".unit."|".address
| sort 0 reg_key _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f earliest(response_values) as earlier_resp latest(response_values) as later_resp by reg_key
| where isnotnull(earlier_resp) AND mvjoin(earlier_resp,",")!=mvjoin(later_resp,",")
| table _time source_h destination_h unit address func earlier_resp later_resp request_values
description: Covert changes to holding registers can alter setpoints or interlocks; comparing matched request/response values highlights tampering distinct from normal operator writes.
value: Covert changes to holding registers can alter setpoints or interlocks; comparing matched request/response values highlights tampering distinct from normal operator writes.
grandmaExplanation: Covert changes to holding registers can alter setpoints or interlocks; comparing matched request/response values highlights tampering distinct from normal operator writes ‚Äî so faults, tampering, and safety issues are spotted right away.
implementation: Deploy Zeek with ICSNPP-Modbus on taps near RTUs; ensure `request_values`/`response_values` are indexed. Focus on critical register ranges from asset documentation; schedule correlation with historian trends for validation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` (`func`, `address`, `unit`, `request_values`, `response_values`, `matched`, `network_direction`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Deploy Zeek with ICSNPP-Modbus on taps near RTUs; ensure `request_values`/`response_values` are indexed. Focus on critical register ranges from asset documentation; schedule correlation with historian trends for validation.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json" matched=true
| where func IN ("0x06","6","0x10","16")
| eval reg_key=destination_h."|".unit."|".address
| sort 0 reg_key _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f earliest(response_values) as earlier_resp latest(response_values) as later_resp by reg_key
| where isnotnull(earlier_resp) AND mvjoin(earlier_resp,",")!=mvjoin(later_resp,",")
| table _time source_h destination_h unit address func earlier_resp later_resp request_values
```

Understanding this SPL

**Modbus Register Value Change Tracking** ‚Äî Covert changes to holding registers can alter setpoints or interlocks; comparing matched request/response values highlights tampering distinct from normal operator writes.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` (`func`, `address`, `unit`, `request_values`, `response_values`, `matched`, `network_direction`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:modbus_detailed:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where func IN ("0x06","6","0x10","16")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **reg_key** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by reg_key** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(earlier_resp) AND mvjoin(earlier_resp,",")!=mvjoin(later_resp,",")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Modbus Register Value Change Tracking**): table _time source_h destination_h unit address func earlier_resp later_resp request_values

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (register_key, old vs new values), Timeline (changes), Line chart (change rate per hour).
visualization: Table (register_key, old vs new values), Timeline (changes), Line chart (change rate per hour).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
